message:
fix(sync): resolve tombstone-over-JSONL semantic with timestamps, wrap restore errors with rebuild-succeeded context

Two coupled fixes for the tombstone-preservation path that "round 17"
flagged as worth-noting-but-not-fixing:

1) Timestamp-aware preservation filter. Previously,
   `tombstones_missing_from_jsonl_tombstones` filtered preserved
   tombstones only by whether the ID appeared as a *tombstone* in the
   JSONL. If the JSONL had the ID as a *non-tombstone* (e.g. someone
   else committed a fresh update for an issue the local user had also
   deleted locally without flushing), the preserved local tombstone
   still won, silently clobbering whatever the JSONL's live record
   represented. That was defensible as "preserve local unflushed
   work", but it meant `br sync --rebuild` could reliably erase
   upstream state in the exact case where a 3-way merge would have
   noticed the disagreement.

   The new semantic uses timestamps: when the JSONL has the ID as a
   non-tombstone, the preserved tombstone only wins if its
   `deleted_at` is strictly newer than the JSONL record's
   `updated_at`. Otherwise the JSONL is considered newer and the
   local tombstone is dropped from the preservation set (the import
   keeps the issue alive). Missing `deleted_at` is treated
   conservatively as "local is newer" so we never silently lose a
   well-formed local deletion.

   Concrete surface change:

     - New `JsonlTombstoneFilter` carries both the tombstone-ID set
       and a `HashMap<String, DateTime<Utc>>` of the JSONL's
       non-tombstone IDs -> updated_at.
     - `scan_jsonl_for_tombstone_filter(path)` parses the JSONL once
       and returns that struct. It replaces the previous
       `get_tombstone_ids_from_jsonl`, which was already a strict
       subset of this functionality.
     - `tombstones_missing_from_jsonl_tombstones(&[...], &filter)`
       applies the three-case filter (already-flushed / JSONL-newer /
       truly-unflushed) and logs a debug line with per-bucket counts
       so operators can see why entries were dropped.

   All four existing callers (startup auto-recovery in
   `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy`, the delegated
   rebuild path in `sync::execute`, the in-place rebuild in
   `execute_import`, and `doctor --repair`) switch to the new filter
   so none of them silently override a newer JSONL non-tombstone with
   a stale local delete.

2) Error wrapping for post-rebuild tombstone-restore failures. Added
   `restore_tombstones_after_rebuild` in `sync/mod.rs`: a thin wrapper
   around `restore_tombstones` that maps any error into a
   `BeadsError::WithContext` whose message makes the failure mode
   unambiguous — "Rebuild from JSONL succeeded, but failed to restore
   N preserved tombstone(s). ... Re-running the command is idempotent
   and safe." All four rebuild surfaces (startup auto-recovery's two
   arms in config, delegation path in sync, `execute_import`'s
   force/rebuild path, and `doctor --repair`) route through this
   wrapper so a transient lock-contention retry exhaustion inside
   `restore_tombstones` no longer surfaces as doctor-repair's generic
   "Repair import failed: ... The JSONL file may be corrupt" (wrong
   root cause), or as a raw fsqlite transaction error at startup
   (uninformative). The bare `restore_tombstones` stays exported for
   callers where the rebuild hasn't completed yet and a rollback is
   still possible; tests of the low-level restore semantics keep
   using it directly.

Tests:

  - `test_tombstones_missing_from_jsonl_tombstones_respects_timestamps`
    pins the new timestamp semantic: an older local tombstone loses
    to the JSONL's live record, a newer one wins.
  - `test_tombstones_missing_from_jsonl_tombstones_only_skips_already_flushed_deletions`
    updated to construct the new `JsonlTombstoneFilter` value instead
    of a bare `HashSet<String>`.
  - `test_scan_jsonl_for_tombstone_filter_rejects_duplicate_issue_ids`
    renamed from the old `get_tombstone_ids_from_jsonl` variant and
    continues to pin the duplicate-ID guard on the scan path.
  - All existing tombstone-preservation e2e tests
    (`e2e_sync_rebuild_preserves_unflushed_tombstones_across_delegation`,
    `e2e_startup_auto_recovery_preserves_unflushed_tombstones`,
    `e2e_doctor_repair_preserves_unflushed_tombstones`) still pass:
    the locally deleted issue's `deleted_at` postdates the JSONL
    flush's `updated_at`, so the "local is newer" branch keeps the
    tombstone and the assertions hold.

Suite: lib 1240, e2e_errors 199, e2e_workspace_commands 172,
e2e_sync_failure_injection 151, repro_3way_merge_bug 138 — all green.
The pre-existing flake in `e2e_rebuilt_alt_db_preserves_fresh_lookup_and_mutation_paths`
(fsqlite "never used pages" / issue #248) is unaffected by this
commit; it fails with the same symptom before the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
